title: License-check tooling (object record ↔ deliverable metadata) enforced in CI
context: M0 relied on a manual structural check plus manual license review. M1 requires automated license-check tooling enforced in CI that validates every deliverable's metadata against its object record. This ensures no description or translation reaches delivery with a missing credit line, incompatible output license, or absent provenance. The tooling builds on schema-001 (content schemas) and rights-001 (rights model policy) and becomes the authoritative CI gate from M1 onward.
objective: Build a license-check script that, for each description and translation deliverable, validates: the required attribution/credit line is present and matches the object record; the output license is present, compatible with the source rights, and not more permissive than the source; any required disclaimer is present; and the provenance record links the deliverable to its object record version. Wire the script into pnpm test so CI fails on any deliverable that fails the check.
acceptanceCriteria: License-check script validates each deliverable's metadata against its object record and fails CI if the credit line, required disclaimer, or provenance record is missing
Script fails CI if the output license is incompatible with or more permissive than the source rights recorded in the object record's factsRights block
Script covers both source-language descriptions and translations; for translations, the output license must also be compatible with the source description's license
Script follows the agent-neutral core pattern: no vendor-specific logic; lives in packages/ or scripts/ consistent with existing project structure
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint all pass; a changeset is added and commits are signed off (DCO)
resources: C:/code/elyos/planning/projects/multilingual-museum-labels/PLAN.md (§7 Data, licensing & compliance; §8 Quality — license check step)
multilingual-museum-labels-schema-001 (content schemas — must be in place)
C:/code/elyos/CLAUDE.md (architecture: no vendor logic in core)
output: PR adding license-check script wired into pnpm test; CI fails on any deliverable with missing/incompatible license, credit line, disclaimer, or provenance
